summary: A Go-based CLI tool with an MCP integration layer and a concurrent test runner, currently undergoing heavy automated test expansion and flakiness mitigation.
concerns: high volume of agent-generated extra test directories suggests potential test flakiness or brittle CI
uncommitted modified and staged files in working tree
heavy reliance on automated test generation (glm-agents) may lead to redundant or low-value test coverage
ongoing documentation of flakiness detector (FEAT-044) indicates existing stability issues
suggestions: consolidate or prune the numerous agent-generated extra test directories to reduce maintenance burden
commit or stash the pending working tree changes to maintain a clean state
focus on stabilizing the stress test engine and flakiness detector before expanding test surface
review wire-generated dependency injection tests for actual value versus boilerplate overhead
